code_07.1: Press twice to open the Quick Chat interface, or once to select the currently highlighted option.
code_07.11: Auto-respond to the last thing in your chat window.
code_07.13: Open the Quick Chat menu and the context menu.
code_07.15: Repeat the last thing you said.
code_07.7: Move back to the previous menu level.
code_07.9: Return to the top level of the menu.

code_07.0:
Welcome to Free Chat|You are free to chat to other users simply by typing. This is fun and you will make lots of new friends, however, it's important to be careful and stay safe. To do so:

-Never give out personal information such as your real name, age, phone number or school.

-Never share your password with anyone except your parents. Someone else might use your password to pretend to be you and do something that may get you into trouble.

-If someone says or does something that makes you uncomfortable, or if someone asks you for personal information, tell your parents and use the 'Report' button to report them to Jagex.

Quick Chat - Navigation Basics|The Quick Chat interface can be triggered either by hitting [ENTER] twice (once to enter chat, again to enter quickchat) or by right-clicking your user name at the bottom of the chat window.

You can navigate the menus by left-clicking on options, by pressing one of shortcut keys listed next to them or by scrolling with [PAGE UP] and [PAGE DOWN] and selecting with [ENTER].

Choosing something with a next to it will open up another set of options in the next column. Anything without a is what you will actually say.
code_07.1:
Quick Chat - Saying Things|Usually, selecting a phrase will automatically make your character say it, however, you will find that some of the things that you can say have a '...' in them. This indicates that you must choose something to complete this phrase. Selecting one of these phrases will either bring up a list of appropriate choices or will prompt you to type in the name of the object you want to talk about.

If you wish to avoid scrolling down a large list simply press the first letter of the option you are looking for and it will jump to the first item on the list beginning with that letter.

You can repeat the last thing you have said using Quick Chat at any time by pressing [F12].
code_07.2: Quick Chat - Further Navigation|You may return to a previous section of the menu by either <col=8080ff>left-clicking<col=ffffff> on it, selecting it on the trail at the top of the interface or by pressing <col=ffff00>[BACK SPACE]<col=ffffff> to move back one menu layer or <col=ffff00>[HOME]<col=ffffff> to return to the beginning.
code_07.3:
Quick Chat - Context Sensitive Chat|The last item on the Quick Chat menu (always the [X] shortcut) is context sensitive, This means that it will change depending on what you are doing. If you are playing a minigame, it will offer chat relevant to the minigame you are playing; otherwise, it will refer to the skill you most recently gained experience in.

You can jump to the context sensitive menu at any time by hitting [F10], even if the Quick Chat interface is not currently open.
code_07.4:
Quick Chat - Auto-responses|If you see a message in your chat log with a next to it, this means that you can auto-respond to it. Right-clicking one of these messages and selecting auto-respond will bring up a list of suitable responses from which to choose. This will save you lots of time!

Hitting [F9] will bring up a list of auto-responses to the last thing in your chat log.
code_07.5:
Quick Chat - Clan Chat and Friends Chat|You can still send private messages and use the Friends Chat and Clan Chat channels for Quick Chat messages.

To send a private message to a friend, simply right-click on their name in your Friends list, and select 'Quick Message' from the menu.

To talk in a Friends Chat or Clan Chat channel, you can right-click your own name in the chatbox, then select 'Friends Quick Chat' or 'Clan Quick Chat'. Alternatively, you can hit [ENTER] then [/] before pressing [ENTER] again to call up the menu.
code_07.6: Quick Chat - Ready To Go|You are now ready to get started with the quick-chat system. You can access a  reference for all the shortcut keys or view this guide again at any time by <col=8080ff>left-clicking<col=ffffff> the [i] icon in the top left hand corner of the quick-chat interface.

code_05.1: [Raptor]	Running. Waste of time, unless you're chasing after a monster.
code_05.11: [Troll]	Hur-hur, running good. Me look forward to running event.
code_05.12: [Thok]	Ha! Yes, my rocky friend, Thok thinks this will be good.
code_05.2: [Thok]	Metal-man, races can be fun. Me and brother Marm would race to kill as many monsters as possible.
code_05.21: [Troll]	How do mermaids run when they have no legs?
code_05.23: [Troll]	Humans also run badly with no legs. That's why I eat legs first.
code_05.3: [Raptor]	That kind of race I can get behind.
code_05.31: [Mr Mordaut]	It's a misconception that athletics is solely about strength and speed.
code_05.32: [Mr Mordaut]	It can be very tactical. The brain is just as important as the feet.
code_05.34: [Mr Mordaut]	So, use your mind to take advantage of your opponents.
code_05.41: [Mr Mordaut]	Some people compare running to a card game, with all the mind games and luck that entails.
code_05.42: [Thok]	Thok like card games. Snap is a big favourite after a glass of mead.
code_05.43: [Mr Mordaut]	I was thinking of a more intricate card game.
code_05.44: [Thok]	Snap with two packs of cards?
code_05.51: [Thok]	Welcome to the Games! I am Thok, champion of Daemonheim, drinker of mead, son of Daddy Thok...
code_05.52: [Mr Mordaut]	Deary me. People are here to watch the games, not hear about you.
code_05.53: [Mr Mordaut]	Welcome. My name is Mr. Mordaut and it is my honour to commentate on this event.
code_05.61: [Mr Mordaut]	Welcome, everyone. My name is Mr. Mordaut and my colleague here is known as ...um...zombie.
code_05.63: [Mr Mordaut]	Indeed, we really have a good turnout today.

code_05.1: [Raptor]	Not bad. I'm impressed, should use your speed to chase down monsters.
code_05.11: [Thok]	A good race, Thok think this was great.
code_05.12: [Troll]	Hur hur, me like fast food.
code_05.13: [Thok]	Thok see what you did there!
code_05.2: [Thok]	Thok agree with metal man. Chase monsters, then crush them with your fists.
code_05.21: [Troll]	Little human ran well. Might be too fast for me to eat them.
code_05.22: [Thok]	No eating the athletes! Thok want to see more races.
code_05.31: [Tentacle]	They run faster than I can swim!
code_05.32: [Troll]	They not run faster than troll can swim.
code_05.33: [Tentacle]	Aren't you made of rock? No troll can swim.
code_05.34: [Troll]	Look at it this way: trolls swim very fast downwards.
code_05.41: [Mr Mordaut]	This athlete really has got their head in the game.
code_05.42: [Zombie]	Zombie go into game, eat head?
code_05.43: [Mr Mordaut]	No, zombie. We can snack at the post-race buffet.
code_05.52: [Mr Mordaut]	I'd say at least gale force 10.
code_05.53: [Thok]	Thok say they have turned it right up to 11!
code_05.61: [Thok]	Thok thought that was...BEST EVENT EVER!
code_05.62: [Mr Mordaut]	Yes, quite. Well done, athlete, a remarkable score that you should be proud of.
code_05.71: [Mr Mordaut]	Well done athlete, a fantastic event and a score to be proud of.
code_05.74: [Thok]	S'victory drink. I shtarted drinkings thish morning jusht incashe. S'good game. Good game.

code_05.1: [Tentacle]	I'd give up now if I were you, human.
code_05.11: [Thok]	Not good so far. Think more like Thok, be more like Thok!
code_05.12: [Tentacle]	You really are full of yourself.
code_05.13: [Thok]	Thok is full of Thok! Fish-woman is correct.
code_05.21: [Tentacle]	Haha, that was a terrible round.
code_05.22: [Thok]	Do not be rude, squishy fishy. Thok don't like rude people.
code_05.23: [Tentacle]	I'm not scared of you, you great oaf.
code_05.25: [Tentacle]	Such pretty butterflies...I think I'll take a nap now.
code_05.31: [Mr Mordaut]	A disappointing last round, but I'm sure our athlete can recover.
code_05.41: [Mr Mordaut]	A disappointing score for our athlete.
code_05.42: [Mr Mordaut]	I'm sure they'll do better in future attempts.

code_07.2: [Thok]	They faster than Thok going through Daemonheim!
code_07.3: [Zombie]	Slow down! Too fast for Zombie!
code_07.4: [Mr Mordaut]	We might see a new Games world record here.
code_07.5: [Tentacle]	How they can go so fast? Someone must be cheating.
code_07.6: [Troll]	A rolling troll gathers no moss!

code_07.1: [Thok]	By Thok's mighty beard, what is that thing?
code_07.5: [Mr Mordaut]	Holy dragonkin, is that a tutu?
code_07.6: [Zombie]	I like my brains like I like my bacon...streaky

code_07.2: [Troll]	Is food falling from sky now?
code_07.3: [Troll]	Someone has a beef wiv dis race

code_07.1: [Thok]	Giving up? Thok fought on with worse injuries.
code_07.2: [Thok]	Ow, Thok think that must have hurt.
code_07.3: [Thok]	Don't give up in face of adversity! Punch it in nose!
code_07.5: [Troll]	If they not in race, can Troll eat them?
code_07.6: [Tentacle]	I've seen sea cucumbers move faster.

code_05.1: [Raptor]	Waste of time. Should be killing things.
code_05.11: [Troll]	Yay, a race! Collect all the shinies, yay!
code_05.2: [Troll]	But look at the pretty shiny rocks and trees.
code_05.22: [Zombie]	Grab stuff...in short space of time...
code_05.3: [Raptor]	I should kill you where you stand, monster.
code_05.31: [Tentacle]	Why are you surface dwellers fascinated with collecting things?
code_05.32: [Mr Mordaut]	It's a compulsion. We dragons are probably the worst of the bunch.
code_05.33: [Tentacle]	You sit on a hoard of gold?
code_05.34: [Mr Mordaut]	Well, yes, but I only collect the rare coins. I have an Avarrocka guinea, would you believe...
code_05.4: [Troll]	Wanna see them collect shiny things. Yay shiny!
code_05.41: [Thok]	Lemme guess, Zombie - you collect brains.
code_05.43: [Thok]	Haha! Thok thinks you just eat brains. This race all about collecting and keeping.
code_05.51: [Raptor]	Where is the killing? Why aren't the competitors hitting each other?
code_05.52: [Mr Mordaut]	It's strictly a non-contact sport.
code_05.53: [Raptor]	Fine. Then why aren't the competitors throwing axes at each other?
code_05.54: [Mr Mordaut]	That's still contact, Raptor. Just...at a distance.
code_05.61: [Mr Mordaut]	Grabbing what you can in a short space of time. A metaphor for life, eh Thok?
code_05.62: [Thok]	I not know metaphor. My family talks with each other, not use flags.
code_05.63: [Mr Mordaut]	Good lord, I didn't mean semaphor! You have a beautiful mind, Thok.

code_07.10: [Mr Mordaut]	It's a frenzy of picking things up!
code_07.11: [Mr Mordaut]	They've missed one there. That might cost them.
code_07.12: [Mr Mordaut]	Use your body and your brain if you want to win the game!
code_07.17: [Thok]	Thok mentally high-fives all of you!
code_07.3: [Raptor]	That would be better in weapon form.
code_07.4: [Troll]	Why do they not eating that?
code_07.5: [Troll]	Is that a good one to get?
code_07.6: [Troll]	Yay! Troll woulda picked dat one!
code_07.7: [Tentacle]	Grab, grab, grab! The time is ticking down.
code_07.8: [Tentacle]	Grab that one...over there! No, the other one!
code_07.9: [Tentacle]	Pick that one up to your left! No, your other left!

code_05.1: [Thok]	A performance to sing tales of! It tough for anyone to top that.
code_05.13: [Raptor]	Don't try to escape me fish. I'll be coming after you.
code_05.2: [Mr Mordaut]	A marvellous display. I've not seen such skill for at least two centuries.
code_05.22: [Mr Mordaut]	Indeed. A great performance from our athlete.
code_05.23: [Raptor]	Now, dragon, time to meet my flail!
code_05.24: [Mr Mordaut]	*Sigh* Oh, do go away, Raptor.
code_05.31: [Mr Mordaut]	Wonderful! A fantastic show. Bravo athlete, bravo!
code_05.41: [Thok]	Haha! A good round! Well done, squishy person!
code_05.44: [Tentacle]	Why are there two of you?
code_05.51: [Thok]	Keep it up, squishy athlete, and you'll be almost as good as Thok.
code_05.61: [Thok]	A good game, you make Thok proud.
code_05.65: [Thok]	Sorry, Thok thought you were about to make rude comment.

code_05.1: [Tentacle]	A pitiful performance. I thought humans were meant to be good at this?
code_05.11: [Tentacle]	Hah! Better luck next time, human.
code_05.2: [Thok]	Thok sure they just limbering up. They must be rusty.
code_05.22: [Mr Mordaut]	It's not that bad. I'm sure that was just a practice game.
code_05.31: [Mr Mordaut]	A disappointing performance from our athlete. I'm sure they can come back and do better.
code_05.32: [Zombie]	Zombie...could do better...with half an arm...
code_05.41: [Raptor]	Not impressed. Seems like a practice round.
code_05.42: [Tentacle]	Or they're just not very good.
code_05.51: [Tentacle]	Not the best score I've ever seen.
code_05.62: [Mr Mordaut]	Come now, my metal-encased friend, I'm sure this was just a practice game.
code_05.63: [Mr Mordaut]	With drive and determination, they'll have a top score in no time.

code_05.1: [Mr Mordaut]	Five Ages. Years of conflict and learning, and our collective culture gives us cheese. Down a hill.
code_05.11: [Troll]	Cheese is rubbish. Milk is better.
code_05.12: [Tentacle]	You're not meant to troll the cheese, you're meant to roll the cheese!
code_05.2: [Thok]	Cheese rolling is Thok's favourite! CHEESE ROLL - CHEESE ROLL - CHEESE ROLL.
code_05.21: [Troll]	Look! Cheese roll much better when it in a circle...
code_05.22: [Tentacle]	You are kidding me. Are you saying that trolls haven't invented the wheel yet?
code_05.23: [Troll]	Troll can think of many uses for it!
code_05.24: [Troll]	After games, Troll go back and tell friends about dis 'wheel'.
code_05.31: [Mr Mordaut]	You a fan of cheese, Zombie? You kick back in the evening with some cheese and crackers?
code_05.33: [Mr Mordaut]	Interesting. Were they moreish? A bit 'Oooh, just one more brain'.
code_05.41: [Raptor]	I refuse to be here while someone rolls cheese.
code_05.42: [Thok]	Why, Raptor? Bad history with cheese?
code_05.44: [Thok]	Eat too much cheese and have bad Raptor nightmare?
code_05.45: [Raptor]	If you'll excuse me, I have strykewyrms to kill.
code_05.51: [Mr Mordaut]	This reminds me of a joke. What did mother bear scream at baby bear when he was running in a cheese rolling race?

code_07.1: [Mr Mordaut]	That could have been feta!
code_07.2: [Mr Mordaut]	Ooh, they really milked that performance.
code_07.3: [Tentacle]	Like the cheese, that performance stank.
code_07.5: [Thok]	Look on bright side: ten-second rule means the cheese is good to eat.
code_07.6: [Thok]	Athlete is clearly merry on mead.

code_07.1: [Mr Mordaut]	That was a gouda performance!
code_07.2: [Mr Mordaut]	You really are the big cheese
code_07.4: [Mr Mordaut]	You better grab some of that cheese because your performance was a cracker!
code_07.5: [Tentacle]	Congratulations! You have a talent for fermented bovine secretions.
code_07.6: [Thok]	Carve that cheese into a crown! No one beats that score.

code_07.0: The vast landmasses and violent history of the western continent have produced many disparate communities, whose main commonality is in their lust for adventure and advancement. Now they are aware of the east, and the ships begin to sail...
code_07.1: The only part of the archipelago where westerners are often seen, and seen in increasing numbers. It is mostly tribal and its khans are mainly fishermen. Forests of wood and bamboo are common.
code_07.2: Parts of the Skull are heavily fortified in fear of an invasion from the west. Khans in this area take advantage of the local gunpowder production to maintain a warlord's status; they tend to mistrust westerners.
code_07.3: Less settled than the Skull. Apart from deposits of black slate, which is quarried by a dispersed population of mining colonies, it is otherwise mostly barren and a fine place for those seeking solitude for whatever reason.
code_07.4: A large island chain covered in cherry trees. The many soothsayers and seasingers of the area often provoke their khans to do war on each other; this and the preponderance of sea monsters make the Scythe dangerous to traverse.
code_07.5: Rich in jade, a draw to pirates and prospectors alike. The khans of the Bowl are more interested in wealth and luxury than in military or political power, an attitude which draws merchants as thickly as flies.
code_07.6: These islands are joined by massive bridges and dams, in a failed attempt to engender togetherness and trust. This place is the centre of political power: a hotbed of intrigue and betrayal. Steel production here bolsters the massive shipping industry.
code_07.7: The islands are protected by a great reef that is very dangerous to navigate. Some say the reef changes shape like it is alive, while others have claimed it is due to geological instability in the area. The pearls recovered from the reef are very valuable.
code_07.8: Not much is known about these far-off islands. Rare koi have been known to congregate in the surrounding waters, and khans send mining expeditions to the islands to find rich veins of minerals and jewels before their rivals.

code_05.26: The Island that Reflects the Moon
code_05.51: The Reef that Lies to Mapmakers
code_05.55: The Lair of Tavi and Vynal
code_05.56: The Island that is Blamed for Nothing
code_05.6: The Islands That Once Were Turtles
code_05.63: The Faraway Place where Things are Kept

code_07.1: If a westerner has met a seasinger, soothsayer or siren, it was likely here. It is a minor centre of study and research for those not enamoured of the civilised east.
code_07.10: A thin strip of land nestled in shallow, warm seas. Mermaids have been known to visit this place in large numbers.
code_07.11: Sulphur is mined here, and gunpowder is made. At the island's height is a massive jade palace, built by Thalassia's khan.
code_07.12: Houses several fishing towns that make strong use of explosives in their fishing. The ruling khan is known to be cruel, especially to his own people.
code_07.13: The only major concentration of the seatouched people, a reasonably fertile island with a single, semi-fortified township.
code_07.14: A living island (likely an evolved sea monster) with a brain at its centre and floating eyeballs as its sensory system.
code_07.15: An island curiously shaped like the shell of a nautilus. Home of a siren seasinger.
code_07.16: Vast columns of black stone erupt from the ground across the length of this island; one can walk from coast to coast without ever touching the soil.
code_07.17: Honazo, a crabbing town, stands in a wide and peaceful bay here.
code_07.18: The scientific and superstitious disagree on what came to form the knuckle-shaped depressions indenting the entire surface of this island.
code_07.19: Named not for their shape or anything that goes on there, but for the proliferation of waterfalls that fill the air with mist and spray.
code_07.2: The sea in the area is teeming with fish and turtles, making it a favourite for whales. One named 'Shuma' is particularly storied.
code_07.20: The sea between these two islands is an unfathomable depth, a rift of stone from which earthquakes are born.
code_07.21: A mining colony originally built to dig for black slate; it has become highly prosperous since discovering large quantities of high-quality gems.
code_07.22: The Chimera Isles are here. Since the straits are used by smugglers, a number of inns and smuggling dens can be found on the isles.
code_07.23: This is where aging sea monsters go to die. Vast bones scatter the coral outcrops as they plunge to the depths.
code_07.24: Swift currents, strong winds and a remarkably stable microclimate make the average temperature of this island lower than its neighbours. Rumours exist of a Purist movement having made it their headquarters.
code_07.25: The island of Haranu is awash with cherry forests. The island was once home to occultists before they were rounded up and executed by the Scythe's khans.
code_07.26: This is an area renowned for its hospitality and hot springs. Beyond the odd cave giant, the island chain is a paradise.
code_07.27: This large island is the home of the soothsayers, where they are educated before they practice their arts elsewhere.
code_07.28: This is a narrow pass that has been the scene of many battles. It is now used by jade traders.
code_07.29: An island mentioned in whispers as a place where the mind is made sharper, the senses clearer and meditation more effective.
code_07.3: A merchant island, where westerners mingle with easterners. The slave trade begins here.
code_07.30: The prevailing winds around this island change daily, some say on the whim of the sea witches who live here.
code_07.31: An island renowned for its geishas, as well as hibiscus gardens and beautiful scenery.
code_07.32: Declared taboo for settlement by an ancient khan; now overgrown with juniper groves and a rare haven for land-dwelling beasts.
code_07.33: Training islands for the Death Lotus assassins. It is also pockmarked with fishing villages, which form a useful cover.
code_07.34: A cave network made bright by large outcrops of quartz and salt. Home to Shuma the whale.
code_07.35: The people of Kei Pi are renowned for their energy and their hedonism, although some say that the vicious group of pirates known as the Pin operate out of a hidden base here.
code_07.36: The pirates of this island have an unusual reputation for decency.
code_07.37: The only known growing location of a medicinal herb of vast potency.
code_07.38: A fishing village built on stilts. Once home to the Whaler; since obliterated. Still home to the Old Man of the Sea, who can psychically link to sea monsters.
code_07.39: An outpost of the khans' power, heavily fortified. Serves as a centre of frontier justice and bureaucracy.
code_07.4: The village of Gongdung is here. They are particularly suspicious of anything inhuman: soothsayers, sirens, etc.
code_07.40: The most populous city of all the Eastern Lands. The city is largely built on the coast, with some smaller buildings built into a mountain.
code_07.41: The diplomatic capital of the Eastern Lands, and the site of the Halls of Reckoning.
code_07.42: On this island stands a great hole that has never been explored. Pirates dump bodies here.
code_07.43: Officially accorded neutral ground, this island is a place of peace. Khans can relax here, if nowhere else.
code_07.44: A heavily fortified island known for the refinement and ambition of its citizens.
code_07.45: A major cultural centre and focus of social gathering.
code_07.46: Headquarters and stronghold of the Death Lotus assassins.
code_07.47: Home of Quin, the seasinger. A small island, surrounded by sea monsters, with jungle and a single palace on four white pillars.
code_07.48: Rumour abounds of a tiny island where two beast-headed sorcerers bind the drowned dead to their service.
code_07.49: The inhabitants of this island are thought of by other Wushanko peoples with a genuine - if occasionally condescending - fondness. They have an undeserved reputation for dyspraxia.
code_07.5: One large and one small island divided by a wide strip of water shallow enough to wade. Its Khan rules from the northern isle.
code_07.50: The great fortress-city in these straits, known as the Bubble of Mists, is the centre of the pearl trade in Wushanko.
code_07.51: Most of the coral formations of this reef lie below the surface; only the unusual formation known as the Spires of Grief is large enough to house its own city.
code_07.52: A faint actinic glow under inky waters is the only sign that wisps inhabit the coral caves honeycombing the stem of this tiny island.
code_07.53: This island chain is named not for the legendary bird-demons but for ten equally legendary local sages known as the Gu.
code_07.54: Adamant is a rare sight in the Eastern Lands; significant deposits on this inhospitable rock give it its name and its wealth.
code_07.55: A jewel of culture within the bleak black rock; it is said that the finest musicians in the East or West can be found here.
code_07.56: Six hundred men and women died exploring this island. All was forgiven when the massive azure lodes were discovered here.
code_07.57: This island is almost entirely unpopulated. Those brave souls who defy this convention contend daily with the indigenous bladewing population.
code_07.58: A man could survive on this island - just about - eating raw seabird eggs and sour lemonberries. It is not recommended.
code_07.59: This island is named for the restaurant and hotel Carlos has set up here. It gets three pearls out of five in most Wushanko tourist guides.
code_07.6: Legend has it that this island chain was a trio of giant sea turtles, turned to stone by unknown forces. Largely uninhabited.
code_07.60: Reginald Wellington-Smythe has built a factory on this island which produces the finest quality gunpowder in all of The Skull.
code_07.61: Hubbub, now something of a hit with the seasingers, is one of few humans allowed to set foot on this secret siren spa resort. It has a proper name - this is just what Hubbub calls it.
code_07.62: It's no wonder this island keeps getting lost! Any that step foot on it forget all sorts of things - past lovers, what they had for breakfast, their keys...even the island's location.
code_07.63: It's a mystery how one of Daemonheim's hidden dungeons found its way this far out into the Eastern Lands, but here it is, just sitting there.
code_07.64: Kami-shima has 'chosen' Sojobo, Kami and Honovi, and now counts them among its guardians. These three have befriended the many shi guardian lions that roam across its land.
code_07.7: Only known home of the eastern cyclops: a race of semi-sentient humanoids who get on well with everyone.
code_07.8: One of the larger eastern islands. Produces gunpowder which is traded with the rest of the Wushanko Isles, and also grows apples. The ruling khan is territorial and hostile towards all westerners.
code_07.9: The island of Hanto is ruled by a tyrannical khan who has closed his borders to trade. As a result, smuggling is rife in the area.

code_05.11: Sulphur is mined here, and gunpowder is made. At the island's height is a massive jade palace, built by Thalassia's Khan before he was killed by the Assassin.
code_05.17: Honazo, a crabbing town, stood in a wide and peaceful bay here. It has been largely destroyed by Shuma.
code_05.19: Named not for their shape or anything that goes on there, but for the proliferation of waterfalls that fill the air with mist and spray. The Missionary sunk the Pride of Chaos here.
code_05.2: The sea in the area is teeming with fish and turtles, making it a favourite for whales. Here, Shuma attacked and stranded the Whaler.
code_05.24: Swift currents, strong winds and a remarkably stable microclimate make the average temperature of this island lower than its neighbours. The Missionary discovered a Purist group's headquarters here.
code_05.25: The island of Haranu is awash with cherry forests. The island was once home to occultists before they were rounded up and executed by the Scythe's Khans; a renegade group returned to the island only to be outed by the Convict.
code_05.26: This is an area renowned for its hospitality and hot springs. Beyond the odd cave giant, the island is a paradise. Wu was killed here by the Assassin.
code_05.28: This is a narrow pass that has been the scene of many battles. It is now used by jade trawlers. Here the Whaler tried to set a blockade against Shuma.
code_05.31: An island renowned for its geishas, as well as hibiscus gardens and beautiful scenery. Oshoru was khan of this island before his death at the Occultist's hands.
code_05.32: Declared taboo for settlement by an ancient khan; now overgrown with juniper groves and a rare haven for land-dwelling beasts. Home only to the mercenary group known as the Lost Men.
code_05.34: A cave network made bright by large outcrops of quartz and salt. Once home to the mighty Shuma.
code_05.36: The pirates of this island have an unusual reputation for decency. They are loyal to Kirau, informer and fence to the Assassin.
code_05.42: On this island stands a great hole that has never been explored. Pirates dump bodies here, and the Occultist sought death within.

code_07.1: Death is a fast and skilled lone fighter. He would run circles around a larger enemy. He primarily uses his own discipline of magic, but is a capable melee combatant.
code_07.2: Chaeldar's size means she can move very quickly, and would be well suited to a large, slow enemy. Thaerisk - although not as experienced as the other fighters - has a wealth of knowledge and can use this to outsmart any less intelligent opponents.
code_07.3: Cres himself is no fighter, as he is slow and fragile. He will use his creations to fight for him, so would be a good match for multiple smaller enemies. He wouldn't be a match for anyone too physically strong.
code_07.4: The Valluta and Fiara are both large creatures, and are slow-moving fighters. They also both have a high magic resistance, so would be well suited against an opponent who specialises in it.

code_07.1: Whoever is coming through this wing is very big, and slow. It sounds like they are using their immense strength to force their way through the rock. Perhaps they don't have the intelligence to try any other way.
code_07.2: It sounds like there are a lot of enemies breaking through here, and the foul stench of the undead can be smelled. Whoever is leading these troops must have an immense talent to undermine death itself.
code_07.3: This enemy sounds large, slow and heavy, but they are using more than their strength to break through. The impact of magic can be heard alongside demonic chanting, so they must have some intelligence.
code_07.4: Whoever is coming through here sounds small and nimble. They seem to be using only magic, so it could be assumed they'll have a high resistance to magic used against them. Their attacks sound extremely aggressive, as if they're beyond determined.

code_07.3: A hammer. It's hit quite a few nails on the head.

code_07.0: Some of our loading equipment has had a part stolen, we need to find a spare part for the turning mechanism.
code_07.1: The sailors love their drink. Surula needs restocking of the usual stuff.
code_07.10: We need to patch a hole in a sail. Find something suitable.
code_07.11: We've received a new anchor, but nothing to attach it to the ship. The inventory for the warehouse said it was sent here.
code_07.12: Food supplies on the voyages can be extended by letting the sailors catch their own meals.
code_07.2: The lines on the ship are always getting damaged. Get some fresh lines so they can secure the cargo.
code_07.3: One of the repairmen dropped a tool into the sea while working. All I know is that he was attempting to nail up a sign.
code_07.4: The sailors have got themselves in a pickle - they've knotted all the rope into a big mess. Find them a tool to help them out.
code_07.5: Someone in the dry dock underestimated how much wood they would need for a job. I think we have some extra in here.
code_07.6: Some of the crews have reported that they need more light on deck during night watch.
code_07.7: Some sailors feel they need to be better armed. We have some spare weapons in here.
code_07.8: Ammo for the ship weapons is running low. I'm sure I saw some being sent up here.
code_07.9: The dry dock workers need some extra tools to correct the length of some misordered planks.

code_07.0:
Costume pieces are awarded based on your score when completing a performance.

Each costume piece worn during your performance will boost the amount of points you receive for the first action in that performance, allowing you to unlock costume pieces that require higher scores.

Costumes do not affect the XP you earn.
code_07.1:
Pay attention to the audience nearest your performance. They will tell you what they want to see! Doing the requested action will gain you more points.

Be careful, though - performing the same action more than once will cause the audience to become bored, losing you points.

The audience's mood will not affect the XP you earn.
code_07.2:
In each performance, you can choose from a range of actions. For example, in the magic performance you can choose from a variety of objects to levitate, alchemise, teleport or blast.

You can attempt any of the actions, but you will have more success performing actions suitable to your level.

You will only receive XP for a successful action once per
level and performance, so it does not pay to repeat actions.
code_07.3:
Performing emotes during your agility, magic or ranged performances can add to your score.

To avoid the audience becoming bored, you should avoid re-using the same emote.

Emotes will not affect the XP you earn during the performance.

code_07.0: <p=23>Dear Diary, My sister believes I'm going mad on this island. I'm a thirty year old man talking to a diary, so maybe I am. She thinks this will help me though.
code_07.1: <p=24>Dear Diary, I spend all day talking to people, but not really talking to them, you know? I suppose you don't, you're an inanimate object. Adventurers come in and we talk, but it all feels so rote. They're not interested in talking to me, they're interested in the contents of their bank. They never take the time to make a connection.
code_07.2: <p=25>Dear Diary, Another day, another few dozen adventurers. Today I decided to be proactive and make the first move. I tried to ask a couple of adventurers how they were doing and what brought them to the island, but they didn't seem especially interested in conversation. One adventurer in particular seemed especially annoyed and kept loudly complaining about having to complete tasks.
code_07.3: <p=26>Dear Diary, Maybe I should get to know the financial advisor better. I'm worried he'll realise that after working in the same building as him for years I still don't know his name. The complaining adventurer from yesterday still traipses through the bank every few hours. It's weird, adventurers have usually moved on by now.
code_07.4: <p=27>Dear Diary, That adventurer is still here and his mood hasn't improved. He spent an hour yelling about cooking shrimp being beneath him. I think Terrova is going to break it to him that he might not have the temperament for adventuring. I hope he takes it alright.

code_07.0:
Quick Chat - Navigation Basics|The Quick Chat interface can be opened by tapping and holding your user name at the bottom of the chat window.

You can navigate the menus by tapping on options.

Choosing something with a next to it will open up another set of options in the next column. Anything without a is what you will actually say.
code_07.1:
Quick Chat - Saying Things|Usually, selecting a phrase will automatically make your character say it, however, you will find that some of the things that you can say have a '...' in them. This indicates that you must choose something to complete this phrase. Selecting one of these phrases will either bring up a list of appropriate choices or will prompt you to type in the name of the object you want to talk about.

If you wish to avoid scrolling down a large list simply press the first letter of the option you are looking for and it will jump to the first item on the list beginning with that letter.
code_07.2: Quick Chat - Further Navigation|You may return to a previous section of the menu by either <col=8080ff>tapping<col=ffffff> on it or selecting it on the trail at the top of the interface.
code_07.3: Quick Chat - Context Sensitive Chat|The last item on the Quick Chat menu (always the <col=ffff00>[X]<col=ffffff> shortcut) is context sensitive, This means that it will change depending on what you are doing. If you are playing a minigame, it will offer chat relevant to the minigame you are playing; otherwise, it will refer to the skill you most recently gained experience in.
code_07.4: Quick Chat - Auto-responses|If you see a message in your chat log with a <img=3> next to it, this means that you can auto-respond to it. <col=8080ff>Tapping and holding<col=ffffff> one of these messages and selecting auto-respond will bring up a list of suitable responses from which to choose. This will save you lots of time!
code_07.5:
Quick Chat - Clan Chat and Friends Chat|You can still send private messages and use the Friends Chat and Clan Chat channels for Quick Chat messages.

To send a private message to a friend, simply tap and hold on their name in your Friends list, and select 'Quick Message' from the menu.

To talk in a Friends Chat or Clan Chat channel, you can tap and hold your own name in the chatbox, then select 'Friends Quick Chat' or 'Clan Quick Chat'.
code_07.6: Quick Chat - Ready To Go|You are now ready to get started with the quick-chat system. You can view this guide again at any time by <col=8080ff>tapping<col=ffffff> the [i] icon in the top left hand corner of the quick-chat interface.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> The day we moved here, Primrose was two. I remember her toddling next to us, carrying a book, one of the bigger ones.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> You couldn't stop her. We called her our little Pack Yak Primrose.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Sure, she slowed us down, made the journey painfully long, but you try to tell her to stop!

code_07.0: <p=310>The day we moved here, Primrose was two. I remember her toddling next to us, carrying a book, one of the bigger ones.
code_07.1: <p=310>You couldn't stop her. We called her our little Pack Yak Primrose.
code_07.2: <p=310>Sure, she slowed us down, made the journey painfully long, but you try to tell her to stop!

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> You can ignore death, blood, murder. The mind's flexible like that. They all happen to far away people with far away lives.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> It was blissful. We lived in a house where those things didn't exist.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> But it was like we were pulling back the curtains and hoping for the best. Hoping that those flimsy pieces of fabric would keep the violence out.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> We were wrong. The world got in. A sea troll got in...

code_07.0: <p=2>You can ignore death, blood, murder. The mind's flexible like that. They all happen to far away people with far away lives.
code_07.1: <p=2>It was blissful. We lived in a house where those things didn't exist.
code_07.2: <p=2>But it was like we were pulling back the curtains and hoping for the best. Hoping that those flimsy pieces of fabric would keep the violence out.
code_07.3: <p=0>We were wrong. The world got in. A sea troll got in...

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Gail didn't come to us at first. She'd been watching us for some time from the Needle, making sure we weren't monsters, I guess.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I mean, we'd seen her. She wasn't great at hiding herself, after all. She's ten-foot tall and blue.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Joss assumed the worst, as he always did. Tried to scare her away with air blasts. Nothing that would hurt her, just to send a message.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> She kept clear. It was only when Joss left - no, abandoned - us that she felt courage enough to talk to me.

code_07.0: <p=2>Gail didn't come to us at first. She'd been watching us for some time from the Needle, making sure we weren't monsters, I guess.
code_07.1: <p=2>I mean, we'd seen her. She wasn't great at hiding herself, after all. She's ten-foot tall and blue.
code_07.2: <p=2>Joss assumed the worst, as he always did. Tried to scare her away with air blasts. Nothing that would hurt her, just to send a message.
code_07.3: <p=2>She kept clear. It was only when Joss left - no, abandoned - us that she felt courage enough to talk to me.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Primrose is so strong, but people can't see past the illness.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Doctors, visitors, they all make that mistake. Judge her by the disease, the microscopic evil magnified into something huge.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> But I see the strength. Damn girl never lets an argument go un-won. She'll fight for the things she loves, for sure.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> And she's full of love. Not always for her mum, but, you know, it's there. Growing up, she loved everything, cared for everything.

code_07.0: <p=300>Primrose is so strong, but people can't see past the illness.
code_07.1: <p=300>Doctors, visitors, they all make that mistake. Judge her by the disease, the microscopic evil magnified into something huge.
code_07.2: <p=310>But I see the strength. Damn girl never lets an argument go un-won. She'll fight for the things she loves, for sure.
code_07.3: <p=310>And she's full of love. Not always for her mum, but, you know, it's there. Growing up, she loved everything, cared for everything.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> My whole life, I've been surrounded by books. Not once have I read one.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I can see my tombstone: 'crushed under a pile of books'.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> When I met Joss, he was hidden behind a stack of wizarding tomes. He'd line our house with them.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Primrose treated them as toys, then things to climb, then adventures to climb into.
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Her imagination was always wild. She'd play out the God Wars with Saradomin as a ladle and Zamorak as a pepper pot.

code_07.0: <p=2>My whole life, I've been surrounded by books. Not once have I read one.
code_07.1: <p=315>I can see my tombstone: 'crushed under a pile of books'.
code_07.2: <p=2>When I met Joss, he was hidden behind a stack of wizarding tomes. He'd line our house with them.
code_07.3: <p=2>Primrose treated them as toys, then things to climb, then adventures to climb into.
code_07.4: <p=2>Her imagination was always wild. She'd play out the God Wars with Saradomin as a ladle and Zamorak as a pepper pot.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Every weekend, it was the same thing. Pack provisions, wrap some books in blessed cloth and worry about leaving Primrose behind.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Then it was the trip to the Wizards' Tower, cart and all. Sell some books to Sloop, ask around about Joss, cook a Weekend Banquet for the elite wizards.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Then it was back home, worried about Primrose and whether Gail had kept her safe.

code_07.0: <p=2>Every weekend, it was the same thing. Pack provisions, wrap some books in blessed cloth and worry about leaving Primrose behind.
code_07.1: <p=2>Then it was the trip to the Wizards' Tower, cart and all. Sell some books to Sloop, ask around about Joss, cook a Weekend Banquet for the elite wizards.
code_07.2: <p=2>Then it was back home, worried about Primrose and whether Gail had kept her safe.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Some memories stick in you like barbs. They hurt whenever you accidentally catch yourself on them.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I...I was on the way back from the Wizards' Tower. The day had gone well: I'd sold some books for more than I expected, and I'd gotten the cooking job.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> But I arrived at the house. There was the bang-bang of the door, slamming in the wind.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I abandoned the cart, rushed in. I called for Primrose. Nothing.
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> A stray sea troll had found its way into the house. It was long gone when I got there, but... It...
code_07.6: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> That was the first time Gail talked to me. I couldn't move, I was crying on the floor of the kitchen. And Gail came to me.

code_07.0: <p=309>Some memories stick in you like barbs. They hurt whenever you accidentally catch yourself on them.
code_07.1: <p=2>I...I was on the way back from the Wizards' Tower. The day had gone well: I'd sold some books for more than I expected, and I'd gotten the cooking job.
code_07.2: <p=302>But I arrived at the house. There was the bang-bang of the door, slamming in the wind.
code_07.3: <p=302>I abandoned the cart, rushed in. I called for Primrose. Nothing.
code_07.4: <p=302>A stray sea troll had found its way into the house. It was long gone when I got there, but... It...
code_07.6: <p=309>That was the first time Gail talked to me. I couldn't move, I was crying on the floor of the kitchen. And Gail came to me.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> 'Temper-temper'. That's what Joss would always say to me. Gods, it would only make me angrier.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Whenever I would hook his nose out of a damned book: 'temper-temper'.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Whenever I called him on his laziness, doing the chores with some cheap magic: 'temper-temper'.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> They were even his last words to me before he left. Wherever the bastard has gone, I imagine him repeating them like a cheap toy.

code_07.0: <p=300>'Temper-temper'. That's what Joss would always say to me. Gods, it would only make me angrier.
code_07.1: <p=300>Whenever I would hook his nose out of a damned book: 'temper-temper'.
code_07.2: <p=300>Whenever I called him on his laziness, doing the chores with some cheap magic: 'temper-temper'.
code_07.3: <p=300>They were even his last words to me before he left. Wherever the bastard has gone, I imagine him repeating them like a cheap toy.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> A year to live. That's all the doctor could give her, holding his hands out upright like a pauper. 'Please miss, this is all I can give you'.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Primrose just half-smiled when the doctor told her. I was so angry at her for brushing it all aside.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I needed her to cry, break down, something. Just react, damn it!
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Each month, I watched helplessly as she got thinner. Her skin became translucent. But you know Primrose: it just meant that her character shone through brighter.
code_07.5: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I just wish...I wish she didn't hide that character away. So often, she reserved it for her creatures. Her monsters.

code_07.4: <p=0>Each month, I watched helplessly as she got thinner. Her skin became translucent. But you know Primrose: it just meant that her character shone through brighter.
code_07.5: <p=0>I just wish...I wish she didn't hide that character away. So often, she reserved it for her creatures. Her monsters.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> When I was younger, I'd fall in love twice a week. It would come to me as easy as a temper, and would disappear just as quick.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I thought I would fall out of love with Joss as quickly as the others. In the end, the love went, but it took a while.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> There was my love for Saradomin, of course. But I wasn't getting love in return. The score wasn't equal.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Primrose has always been the exception. To fall out of love with Primrose would be to fall out of love with, well, everything.
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I know what you're thinking. No - that time doesn't count. I wasn't angry with Primrose. I was angry at THEM.

code_07.0: <p=310>When I was younger, I'd fall in love twice a week. It would come to me as easy as a temper, and would disappear just as quick.
code_07.1: <p=2>I thought I would fall out of love with Joss as quickly as the others. In the end, the love went, but it took a while.
code_07.2: <p=2>There was my love for Saradomin, of course. But I wasn't getting love in return. The score wasn't equal.
code_07.3: <p=310>Primrose has always been the exception. To fall out of love with Primrose would be to fall out of love with, well, everything.
code_07.4: <p=300>I know what you're thinking. No - that time doesn't count. I wasn't angry with Primrose. I was angry at THEM.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> There was a day when the illness hit her hard. A day when she couldn't ignore it.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Primrose dropped her favourite book: the Adventures of Hannibus, I think it was. She couldn't pick it up. It was open on its back page, the ending staring right up at her.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> It was the first time I'd seen her cry since the illness. I tried to hand it to her, but she shoved it away. I felt like her jailer.

code_07.0: <p=0>There was a day when the illness hit her hard. A day when she couldn't ignore it.
code_07.1: <p=0>Primrose dropped her favourite book: the Adventures of Hannibus, I think it was. She couldn't pick it up. It was open on its back page, the ending staring right up at her.
code_07.2: <p=0>It was the first time I'd seen her cry since the illness. I tried to hand it to her, but she shoved it away. I felt like her jailer.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Is there anyone more self-involved than a wizard?
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I get it, I do. As a wizard, you have to study for years. You have to find a niche that no wizard has found. You have to fight over whatever resources there are.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> But none of them - not Joss, not Sloop, not the Wizards' Tower - can see how self-centred they are. The Tower's just a chocolate box of egos, all in a variety of flavours.

code_07.0: <p=2>Is there anyone more self-involved than a wizard?
code_07.1: <p=2>I get it, I do. As a wizard, you have to study for years. You have to find a niche that no wizard has found. You have to fight over whatever resources there are.
code_07.2: <p=2>But none of them - not Joss, not Sloop, not the Wizards' Tower - can see how self-centred they are. The Tower's just a chocolate box of egos, all in a variety of flavours.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I guess we should have stopped it earlier? Even when she was young, she'd have imaginary friend after imaginary friend.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> They'd even have relatives! Imaginary sisters and brothers would come to visit. Some would get lost, and we'd have to pretend to look for them.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> It got serious once Primrose got ill. She would keep their counsel, rather than talk to me.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> She did it when I wasn't watching, but I knew. She would write poems with them, sing with them, confide in them, ask them for their thoughts.

code_07.0: <p=2>I guess we should have stopped it earlier? Even when she was young, she'd have imaginary friend after imaginary friend.
code_07.1: <p=2>They'd even have relatives! Imaginary sisters and brothers would come to visit. Some would get lost, and we'd have to pretend to look for them.
code_07.2: <p=308>It got serious once Primrose got ill. She would keep their counsel, rather than talk to me.
code_07.3: <p=0>She did it when I wasn't watching, but I knew. She would write poems with them, sing with them, confide in them, ask them for their thoughts.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Saradomin. This is my final prayer.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> If you're listening, then damn you. You choose to listen now? You choose the moment when I give this up? Damn you back to the Third Age.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> You know what? I don't care whether you listen or not. I've found someone else to have faith in.

code_07.1: <p=300>If you're listening, then damn you. You choose to listen now? You choose the moment when I give this up? Damn you back to the Third Age.
code_07.2: <p=300>You know what? I don't care whether you listen or not. I've found someone else to have faith in.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Sloop was my husband's apprentice. He was a boy, not much older than Primrose, I guess. But he'd caught the wizarding bug: self-centred and ambitious.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Still, he kept an ear out for news about Joss. Plus he bought my books - as often as he could spare the money. I ferried them in small batches, as he could only buy so many.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> You could tell he never found it easy talking to me. He wanted to keep our meetings as quick as possible. The boy has a dark secret, you can be sure of it.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Last trip, he asked for the Adventures of Hannibus, and said he would pay well.
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> It's Primrose's favourite book. I...I don't know if I can take it from her. But we need to eat. We need her medicines.

code_07.0: <p=0>Sloop was my husband's apprentice. He was a boy, not much older than Primrose, I guess. But he'd caught the wizarding bug: self-centred and ambitious.
code_07.1: <p=0>Still, he kept an ear out for news about Joss. Plus he bought my books - as often as he could spare the money. I ferried them in small batches, as he could only buy so many.
code_07.2: <p=0>You could tell he never found it easy talking to me. He wanted to keep our meetings as quick as possible. The boy has a dark secret, you can be sure of it.
code_07.3: <p=0>Last trip, he asked for the Adventures of Hannibus, and said he would pay well.
code_07.4: <p=0>It's Primrose's favourite book. I...I don't know if I can take it from her. But we need to eat. We need her medicines.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> You've got to remember, up to this point Gail was a boogeyman. Someone who watched us and then sped away into the night.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Then she's in my house, talking to me, offering me wishes like I was a Princess from one of Primrose's books.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Gail led me to the hilltop. Her hands were so cold and hard. I felt numb, but I couldn't figure out if it was from me or her.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> She showed me something called the Needle. Gail asked again, did I really want my wish? She had to shake me to get an answer.
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> The Needle would take us both back 30 days. While Gail and I would remember what had happened, no one else would. We'd be the only ones to remember the sea troll's attack.
code_07.6: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I agreed, and Gail pulled thread from the Needle. She separated the thread into strands, and a dome of fabric fell about us.
code_07.7: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> The dome flashed with blinding light. You couldn't see about you, it was impossible. As the light faded, Gail gestured me to my house and said 'Be with her'.
code_07.8: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I rushed through the door and checked the calendar. 30 days back - my daughter's birthday. I checked my bedroom. There wasn't another Megan in there.
code_07.9: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Then I went to Primrose. She was asleep in her bed. I sat in the chair, watching her. No one was going to budge me from that spot. Not ever.

code_07.6: <p=2>I agreed, and Gail pulled thread from the Needle. She separated the thread into strands, and a dome of fabric fell about us.
code_07.7: <p=2>The dome flashed with blinding light. You couldn't see about you, it was impossible. As the light faded, Gail gestured me to my house and said 'Be with her'.
code_07.8: <p=310>I rushed through the door and checked the calendar. 30 days back - my daughter's birthday. I checked my bedroom. There wasn't another Megan in there.
code_07.9: <p=310>Then I went to Primrose. She was asleep in her bed. I sat in the chair, watching her. No one was going to budge me from that spot. Not ever.

code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> But I couldn't help buying potion after potion. Near half of the money we had, it was spent on these little moments of strength.

<col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I opened the book on the way to selling it. I couldn't tell you why - I never had an impulse to read a book before.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I guess...I guess I was interested in why Primrose had so much love for it.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Inside was a slip of paper. A poem, lightly scrawled in feeble handwriting. Primrose confessed her love. She begged to be saved.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> The world fell away from me. I stopped the cart, and turned around. My head thrummed with rage. I worked the horse near to death.
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> The fury was wild and directionless. I burned with rage at myself, then the flames moved to Joss. After all, everything bad came from that damned man.
code_07.5: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Finally, the fury rested on the books.
code_07.6: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> When I returned home, I threw them all into the fireplace. Primrose screeched from her bed, begging me to stop.
code_07.7: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I lit the fireplace, and the books burned. But the magic on the books was fuel, and flame mushroomed out of the pit, knocking me to the floor.
code_07.8: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> I was scarred and drowsy. Primrose was screaming about her friends burning, over and over.
code_07.9: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Gail found me, probably thanks to Primrose. She applied some kind of salve to me, but there was no removing the scar. It was to be my brand.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> It was the morning after I traveled back in time. 30 days. Back to the morning of Primrose's birthday.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Primrose looked so much better than I remembered.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Megan:</col> Here she was, unmarked by any sea troll, happy and soulful. She was alive, dear Gods. And happy!

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> There's a story in one of the books. It was the only one mum liked to read to me.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> It's about a giant, Dubbo, who keeps a flock of sheep. He loves those sheep: force-feeds them hay; throws them from hand to hand to make sure they wake up;
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> taps them on the head with his club, to make sure they go to sleep.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Each day, one runs away. He can't understand why - he treats them so well! So, he eats that runaway sheep - in one go, pop! - to send a message to the others.
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> But they keep running away, and he keeps eating them, pop pop pop! Eventually, only one remains.
code_07.5: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> That sheep, Baba, puts up with the feeding, the throwing, the taps on the head. Does nothing. And Dubbo sits down in front of that sheep.
code_07.6: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> 'What's wrong with you, little runt?', the giant asks. But Baba won't answer, and won't run away.
code_07.7: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> 'Why won't you run away?', said Dubbo. He didn't know it, but he wanted the sheep to run away.
code_07.8: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> So he goes out, gets into a fight with another giant and gets eaten himself. It's an odd story.
code_07.9: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Still, I like having Baba around the house as one of my friends. She visits and we chat. She's a real gossip, you know?

code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> It's a big old bag of lies. It stinks. Not once did she look me in the eye. Gah! She's such an awful liar!

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> I've never really had a problem with being bored. I read, I write. And there's always my friends.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> My friends were a gift from my dad. A little apology before the utter scumbag ran away. When I read a book, the character pops into existence. But no one else can see them.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Some of them I don't care much for. I've learned to keep them locked in their books. But Thok, and the others - I couldn't be without them.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Doing anything for more than a few minutes is the ultimate pain in the backside. My lungs burn, my knees go to jelly. I just fall over and want to go to sleep.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Mum used to get medicines. It took all of our money to do it, but I wasn't going to convince her to stop. Megan's the only one who changes Megan's mind.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> The medicines would let me leave the bed, for a few days at least. And it would bring a smile to my mum! Then I'd be back to bed like a brick fell on me.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> I had the idea of stealing some of the medicine for myself. You know, when mum wasn't there. But she rationed it so carefully!
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> And we can't afford it now, anyway. Not now mum's given up the job at the Wizards' Tower.
code_07.5: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Huh. I wonder if I could make a simple strength potion? Just your average, home-made strength potion. Would that work?

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Sloop was the one who brought the news to us. Dad had run away from the Wizards' Tower. Something about plagiarism.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> He was due to go on trial that week, but he'd run from it.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Dad was always a reader, never one to come up with ideas himself. I guess...I guess he just stole ideas from one of his old tomes.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> So, he left us to fend for ourselves, left me with mum. Just as I became ill.
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> The worst part was Sloop. Without dad, there was no reason for Sloop to be at the house any more. Why would dad's apprentice visit when dad wasn't there?
code_07.5: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Mum wouldn't let him in. My love. My Sloop. So he got work at the Wizards' Tower, and passed notes in the books that mum sold to him. But even that's gone now.
code_07.6: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> I know that Sloop won't give up on me. I know he will come for me.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Gilly Willikers had a series of books, but I only ever kept one. It was Gilly Willikers and the Time Key, the first one.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> I liked Gilly. He never treated my illness seriously. He'd walk around with wobbly arms and legs, pretending to be me. I needed that.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> But Gilly had become serious. It was only after pushing him that I found out why.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> He knew something, and my other friends had to push him to tell me.
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> He said that he had found a diary - my mum's diary - in her cloak and had opened it, hoping to find a new friend for me to play with.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Hannibus was the first to call her the Shade, the name that my friends use, even though I tell them not to.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> She would leave most evenings, up to the hilltop, and Hannibus would watch for as long as he could, before he reached the limit of his magic.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Always she would be in the same cloak, always she would head up the hill. The cloak would make her look like a shade, and each day she would grow more hunched.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> It was the 30th: the day of The Return, whatever in all Gielinor THAT was.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> I resolved to follow mum on her nightly trip out of the house. Hannibus gave me his lance, Thok his shield.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> I knew they would both disappear as soon as I left the house, but I thanked them anyway.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> The door closed as my mum left. I pulled a dark cloak over my shoulders, took a swig for strength, and then stepped out into the darkness.
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Mum had a small firebrand, which made her easy to follow, but I had no such luxury. I was locked to darkness. Every sound I made, I feared she would turn around. But she only had eyes for the hilltop.
code_07.5: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> We passed something like a grave, but I had no time to dilly-dally, mum was many steps ahead of me. I could feel the strength in me already sapping.
code_07.6: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Mum reached the top, and I hid behind a berry bush on the edge of the clearing. A tall blue woman called Gail was waiting for my mother.
code_07.7: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> They talked, and I could only make out fragments. And then Gail danced without music.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> I returned to the house, flinging the cloak under my bed. My legs trembled with the night's effort.

code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Then mum walked in, her silhouette filling and darkening the room. My friends retreated to their books, as if mum could see them.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Mum leaned over me, kissed my cheek and whispered 'Happy Birthday, Little Primrose' before turning to leave.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> There was one story I never read. It was about a demon: a hateful thing.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> This demon would thrive on the misery of others. But not by consuming that evil, or anything like that.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> She was miserable herself. And she would bring others into her own misery, so that she could share it. She would bask in that shared misery.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> But to know her was to defeat her. To say her name was to stab her through the heart.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> I took a dose of strength potion and, completely inelegantly, made my way to mum's bedside table, just as Gilly had told me.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> I couldn't quite understand what I was reading. On each day of the diary, there were multiple entries, written in tiny text and boxed off from each other.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> I looked for yesterday's date. Again, there were multiple entries for one day.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> One was familiar: it talked about the pie she had made, and noted that she thought I looked healthier having eaten it.
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> But I didn't recognise the other entries for yesterday; they were events I didn't recognise. An argument we never had. A visit to the Wizards' Tower. A burning of the pie.
code_07.5: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> Then I looked at tomorrow's entry: the 30th. The book was completely blank after that date.
code_07.6: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> And every entry on the 30th, of which there were many, was the same. The Return. The Return. The Return. The Return. The Return.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Gail:</col> I was born as I am now. I didn't have a mother, father or childhood. I was me, nothing more, and I came to... next to the Needle.
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Gail:</col> I knew what The Needle was, but I knew next to nothing about myself. It's a back-to-front way of learning about the world.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Gail:</col> I knew how to use The Needle, what its limits were, how it used time. But I had no idea about people, the world, nothing.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Gail:</col> I'm learning fast and leaving a trail of mistakes behind me, I am sure of it.

code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Gail:</col> I've tried to move on and away, but they've needed me. I've felt responsible. I've wanted to see them happy. And - if I'm honest -  I've felt happy that they needed me.

code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> For reasons I've never understood, you've always been there for us. And we both know that you've seen me at the berry bush. For these reasons, I owe you the truth.
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> When I found out what mum was doing, turning back time to keep me alive, I struggled with what to do. I thought about confronting her, and telling her to stop.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> To let me die. To enjoy the moments left with me.
code_07.4: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> But I knew that wouldn't stop her. She would find a moment when I was asleep to go back in time, to begin another loop without me being aware.
code_07.5: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> There was only one thing I could do. A necessary evil. I couldn't let mum know that I was aware of the Returnings, so I determined to follow her through every time loop.
code_07.6: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> I hope you understand why I am doing this. Please, don't reveal me or my plan. Let me keep coming to the berry bush.
code_07.7: <col=ff0000>Primrose:</col> If not for me, do this for mum. I do all of this for her. She needs to break this loop so that she can live herself.

code_07.0: <p=2>Joss, Primrose and Megan weren't the first people I had encountered.
code_07.1: <p=2>There were the people of the Colony to the east. But they were a fearful people, and hid behind their walls.
code_07.2: <p=2>There were the smaller people of the south-east. They were quick to their pikes, and pushed me back into the treeline.
code_07.3: <p=2>Men and women would occasionally stray close to me, but they were so caught up in their hunting and woodcutting that I didn't want to interrupt.
code_07.4: <p=2>But there was something different about the family. They seemed to have this happy energy, and I wondered what it would be like to share in that.

code_07.0: <col=ff0000>Gail:</col> Is there death in my future? Can I die?
code_07.1: <col=ff0000>Gail:</col> Or am I more kin to the Needle than Primrose or Megan?
code_07.2: <col=ff0000>Gail:</col> Whatever the answer, I feel like I have more stories in me. I don't feel like this is the end.
code_07.3: <col=ff0000>Gail:</col> Something tells me that this is not the beginning either.
